items.title: Max India: Building an Integrated Senior Care Ecosystem in India Max India, through Antara Senior Care, is positioning itself beyond a traditional sen...
items.description_html:
Max India: Building an Integrated Senior Care Ecosystem in India

Max India, through Antara Senior Care, is positioning itself beyond a traditional senior living player.

The company is gradually building an integrated ecosystem around elderly care — combining assisted living, emergency medical support, holistic wellness, insurance, financial literacy, quality standards and clinical governance.

One key initiative is the pilot of an integrated wellness clinic, where modern medicine for emergency care is being combined with Ayurveda, acupressure and other wellness practices.

This matters because senior care is not just about providing residences. As people age, their needs become multi-dimensional — frailty, muscle loss, fall risk, memory care, assisted care, emergency preparedness and emotional wellness all become important.

Max India is also working with government bodies and ministries on senior care advocacy, including submissions to NITI Aayog on insurance and standardisation. This shows that the company is not only building facilities, but also helping shape the broader senior care framework in India.

Another important milestone is Antara becoming the first assisted living provider in India to secure NABH care home accreditation, with 2 care homes now certified.

For Max India, the opportunity is clear:

Senior care = Living + Healthcare + Wellness + Insurance + Standards + Trust.

This integrated approach could help the company differentiate itself in India’s evolving senior care market and build a scalable ecosystem around ageing with dignity. 🏡

But It's not a fresh issue.

It's a Share Swap Deal where Lloyd bought SISCOL, and MK Ventures already has stake in SISCOL from the early days.

Story Time: LLOYD X SISCOL DEAL DECODED

To everyone's surprise

DAM Filed for IPO of SISCOL worth 96Crs Fresh Issue in Jul-25 + OFS upto 1.42Cr Equity Shares
Recd Approval in Nov-25 from SEBI

SISCOL is started by Ex-Jindal Steel & Power Guys
Ravikant Uppal and Rajagopal Kannabiran

Madhu along with other investor invested in 2018 when company was formed at face value of Rs. 10

Post that in 2022 at Rs. 118 per share

Current Order Book: 8,000Cr

Approval of Investment in Lloyds Advance Defence Systems Limited(Defence Arm)

Last Year company raised 987Cr from Right Issue at Price 32 Rs Per Share.

items.title: Goldiam International : Update on ORIGEM ORIGEM doubled its store count & reached 24 operational stores across 12 cities Out of these, 12 stores were ...
items.description_html:
Goldiam International : Update on ORIGEM

ORIGEM doubled its store count & reached 24 operational stores across 12 cities

Out of these, 12 stores were opened in the last 60 days, which means a large part of the store network is still in the early ramp-up phase

ORIGEM reported Q4FY26 REV of 5.6 Cr, while April sales crossed 3.5 Cr

Management is targeting exit monthly REV of 7 Cr by FY27

Company plans to add 8-10 more ORIGEM stores by September 2026 & targets 45-50 COCO stores by exit FY27

Store economics are gradually improving. Mature stores are largely crossing the monthly breakeven level of 18-20 lakh

Capex outlay to open one ORIGEM store is around 3.5 Cr. This includes around 2.5 Cr inventory, 50-60 lakh capex for furniture & fit-outs & 35-40 lakh refundable rental deposit

For mature stores, Management is targeting monthly REV of 35 lakh, with potential to move towards 40-45L over time. Payback period is expected to be around 3 years or lower

Goldiam is also adding sales enablers to improve store throughput. These include a 360 degree digital ring builder, old gold exchange, purchase plans & lower caratage gold jewellery etc



Equity Insights Elite: Goldiam Int

India’s exclusive lab-grown jewellery retailer under the brand name 'ORIGEM'

6 operational stores in Mumbai

Largest COCO LGD retail brand in India

Plans are underway to expand into Delhi NCR and Bengaluru

Glimpse of stores ⏬

items.title: Interesting development from Amber Enterprises Amber Enterprises has entered into a manufacturing collaboration with OPPO Mobiles India. The agreement...
items.description_html:
Interesting development from Amber Enterprises

Amber Enterprises has entered into a manufacturing collaboration with OPPO Mobiles India.

The agreement covers three brands - OPPO, OnePlus and Realme.

This is a major strategic move for Amber as it helps the company diversify beyond its core room air conditioner business.

Manufacturing will be done at an existing OPPO facility under a sub lease arrangement. This keeps the model asset light & the initial capex requirement will be minimal, below 50 Cr.

Production timeline:
Trial production - Q4FY27
Commercial production - Q1FY28

Amber expects to start with 8 million units in the first year of operations & scale it up to 13-15 million units in the second year.

The business may be structured either as sale purchase or job work, depending on OPPO India’s decision.

Management clearly stated that the top line can vary depending on the structure, but the bottom line economics will remain intact.

This is a low margin but high volume business. Industry EBITDA margin, excluding PLI, is around 1.5-2%.

However, the business is expected to be highly capital efficient because the working capital requirement is very low, generally around 5 days.

Management also mentioned that this kind of business can generate ROCE of more than 30-35% on a standalone basis.

Amber will start with assembly and SMT operations.

From the second year onward, company plans to add HDI printed circuit boards once its HDI plant is ready.

Over the next 5-6 years, it aims to increase local value addition to around 30-35%, with potential to move higher depending on the component roadmap.

Amber already manufactures around 9-10 Mn smartwatches annually, around 50 Mn printed circuit board assemblies & more than 5.5 Mn room air conditioner units.

Management compared this with Amber’s RAC journey, where the company started with basic manufacturing & gradually added components like heat exchangers, injection molding, motors, inverter PCB boards, fans & blades.

items.title: Impact of Telegram ban🙃 - VPN demand in India seeing a sharp spike Downloads of major VPN apps rose 49%, moving from a recent daily average of 139,...
items.description_html:
Impact of Telegram ban🙃 -

VPN demand in India seeing a sharp spike

Downloads of major VPN apps rose 49%, moving from a recent daily average of 139,000 to 208,000.

Proton VPN downloads on Apple’s App Store in India jumped 113%, while Turbo VPN downloads rose 85%.

On Google Play, Proton VPN downloads were up 64%, while Turbo VPN downloads increased 35%.

This surge also pushed VPN apps higher in India’s app store rankings

Whenever there is uncertainty, restrictions, privacy concerns or access related issues, VPN demand tends to spike quickly

items.title: MFI sector is showing signs of recovery Collection efficiency has improved meaningfully across players, fresh slippages are reducing & disbursement mo...
items.description_html:
MFI sector is showing signs of recovery

Collection efficiency has improved meaningfully across players, fresh slippages are reducing & disbursement momentum is gradually coming back

Most companies are now following stricter guardrails around borrower exposure, number of lenders & repayment capacity

This is making growth more disciplined compared to the earlier cycle

CreditAccess, Satin, Arman, Spandana, Muthoot Microfin have all indicated improvement in collection trends & asset quality

Some players have already started seeing credit cost normalisation

Disbursement momentum is also coming back

Arman’s MFI Q4 disbursement grew 88% YoY & 62% QoQ.

CreditAccess Q4 disbursement grew 44% QoQ

Most important structural trend is diversification

MFI companies no longer want to remain pure JLG lenders

CreditAccess is expanding into retail finance, individual business loans, mortgage backed loans & 2W finance

Satin wants non MFI AUM to reach 30% by 2030

Muthoot Microfin wants non-JLG portfolio to reach 47% by 2030

Arman is building MSME, LAP, 2W & solar loans

Spandana is also looking at individual loans & micro-LAP

This diversification will reduce volatility in future cycles

items.title: RT Sarang.B: Let me explain PMS to you in the simplest way possible. A very basic Google search + simple math will tell you most PMS products are just...
items.description_html:
RT Sarang.B
Let me explain PMS to you in the simplest way possible.

A very basic Google search + simple math will tell you most PMS products are just exclusivity wrapped in smart-sounding jargon.

Heavy fees.
Frequent underperformance.
“Smart money” marketing.
Luxury positioning.

The business model is brilliant for fund managers.

In many cases, PMS is simply a very sophisticated way of transferring wealth from investors’ pockets to fund managers’ pockets.

The richer the narrative gets, the poorer the investor often becomes.

Retail investors think they’re buying alpha.

Most are just buying expensive storytelling.


Dhirendra Kumar: @sandeepjethwani The pitch reads well. The math underneath does not.
The PMS fee sits on top of the mutual fund expense ratio, not in place of it. All-in: 1.1 to 2%. A direct MF portfolio does the same job at 0.5 to 1%.
Tax pass-through is a feature of mutual funds, not the PMS wrapper. The

RT Ankur Bisen
23 people died in the recent Hauz-Raani fire tragedy in Delhi and a cook was arrested and denied bail for the crime.

Modern cities are a post-colonial reality of India and one that India does not have the wherewithal to deal with. This sits at the heart of the issue and what better way to hide it than to arrest the poor cook as the fall-guy, I argue in this piece for @thewire_in

items.description_html:
While RBI and the government have done a lot to make Indian government bonds attractive to foreign investors, they are not in favor of shifting settlement to overseas platforms like Euroclear.

Euroclear settlement could (or so the argument goes) allow for a wider pool of FPIs to access Indian debt more easily, but the RBI believes it would fragment liquidity.

Reuters @GopikaGopa reports.

items.title: El Niño doesn't hit crops first. It hits the banks that lent to them. A 64% rainfall deficit in early June 2026 is already forcing water cuts. Agri-c...
items.description_html:
El Niño doesn't hit crops first. It hits the banks that lent to them.

A 64% rainfall deficit in early June 2026 is already forcing water cuts. Agri-credit growth will slow, and asset quality goes with it.

The catch is concentration: RRBs run 62% of their book in agri loans, SFBs 23%. PSBs own 61% of the market by share. The rains decide who restructures.

items.title: The last time the RBI did this, FCNR(B) deposits tripled. 2013's hedging concessions took the pile from ~$15bn to $45bn in three years. The same toolk...
items.description_html:
The last time the RBI did this, FCNR(B) deposits tripled.

2013's hedging concessions took the pile from ~$15bn to $45bn in three years. The same toolkit is back, rate caps scrapped, hedging cheapened, off the books from $33.8bn today.

CRR/SLR-exempt, so it's cheap liquidity that pads bank margins. History rhymes.

items.title: You google your blood sugar, get advice from a top hospital, and neither the hospital nor Google actually wrote it. A bot did. And it's wrong So a hea...
items.description_html:
You google your blood sugar, get advice from a top hospital, and neither the hospital nor Google actually wrote it. A bot did. And it's wrong

So a healthcare consultant audited around 500 articles from India's biggest hospital chains, Apollo, Max, Fortis, the lot. Nearly half had a serious error in them.

The kicker: a lot of it was clearly AI-written. One article translated "menstrual discharge" as "sewage water." Another mixed up the mineral iron with the thing you press your shirts with.

And then Google's AI scrapes these pages and serves them back to you as trustworthy, because hey, it came from a famous hospital.

On a real effective basis the INR is 3.7 standard deviations below its decade trend, the most undervalued it's been in years.

items.title: Running on Empty India's 166 major reservoirs have drained to 27.5% of capacity. 113 of them now sit at 40% or below, and just 1 in 5 are more than ha...
items.description_html:
Running on Empty

India's 166 major reservoirs have drained to 27.5% of capacity. 113 of them now sit at 40% or below, and just 1 in 5 are more than half full.

The monsoon is running 40% short nationally and a brutal 63% behind in Central India. The South is the thirstiest region of all at 21%.

items.title: RT The Kobeissi Letter: Absolutely incredible. SpaceX, $SPCX, is now trading above $220/share in overnight trading. This makes SpaceX worth nearly $2....
items.description_html:
RT The Kobeissi Letter
Absolutely incredible.

SpaceX, $SPCX, is now trading above $220/share in overnight trading.

This makes SpaceX worth nearly $2.9 TRILLION, less than $100 billion away from surpassing Microsoft.

This also puts SpaceX up +63% from its IPO price of $135/share.

Furthermore, the combined market cap of both SpaceX and Tesla is now at a record $4.4 trillion.

That’s bigger than the market cap of Apple and roughly equivalent to the market cap of Google.

To top it all off, SpaceX raised a record $85.7 billion after underwriters exercised the "greenshoe" overallotment.

Never in history has an IPO ever come remotely close to what we are seeing right now.

Truly incredible.

items.title: HCL Tech's $151 mn cheque for a 10.5% stake in Sarvam will be most likely be the largest direct investment in a startup by an IT services co. Sarvam a...
items.description_html:
HCL Tech's $151 mn cheque for a 10.5% stake in Sarvam will be most likely be the largest direct investment in a startup by an IT services co.

Sarvam also had operating revenues of Rs 45.1 cr in FY26 vs Rs 1.5 cr in FY25.

Clients include SBI Life, LIC, IDFC, Tata Cap and CRED.


The Arc: 🚨 Sarvam has raised $234 million from IT services major HCL Technologies along with Silicon Valley venture firm Bessemer.

This is part of a larger $300 million funding round.

Sarvam is now valued at $1.5 billion, as compared to about $200 million last year.

🚨 Razorpay has made a confidential filing with market regulator Sebi for its IPO, according to a notice.

The payments company has taken board approval to raise Rs 2,700 crore ($285 mn) in primary capital in addition to offer for sale.

Razorpay was last valued at $7.5 billion.
